purpose: Expand the existing Ananta TUI MVP into a broad operator/developer terminal surface that reuses existing Angular/Hub APIs where practical, without becoming an unsafe duplicate control plane.
goal: The TUI should cover most daily Ananta frontend workflows: dashboard/status, goals, tasks, artifacts, knowledge, config, teams/blueprints, automation, audit, approvals and repair visibility, using terminal-appropriate screens and safe backend-gated actions.
success_criteria: TUI provides navigable top-level sections similar to the Angular frontend surface.
TUI reuses existing Hub APIs/read models instead of adding duplicate backend logic.
Users can inspect and operate common Goals/Tasks/Artifacts workflows without browser.
Users can inspect System/Config/Audit/Automation state from terminal.
Dangerous writes remain backend-permissioned, explicit and auditable.
Deep/complex operations degrade to browser fallback where terminal UX would be unsafe.
TUI has smoke tests and view-model tests for all major sections.
architecture_guardrails: No planning, routing, approval, repair execution or policy logic is duplicated in TUI.
All writes use existing backend APIs and preserve backend validation/audit.
Read-heavy first; write actions require confirmation and risk labels.
Secrets, tokens and sensitive config values are redacted.
Browser fallback remains available for complex admin/configuration screens.

tasks.title: Frontend API surface mapping create
acceptance_criteria: Map Angular HubApiService methods to TUI sections.
Classify each feature as TUI-MVP, TUI-advanced, browser-fallback or not-suitable-for-terminal.
No existing frontend API is reimplemented blindly without UX/risk decision.
Mapping identifies missing or unstable endpoints, if any.

tasks.title: TUI section navigation shell expand
acceptance_criteria: TUI has top-level navigation for Dashboard, Goals, Tasks, Artifacts, Knowledge, Config, System, Teams, Automation, Audit, Repair and Help.
Keyboard navigation is consistent.
Current section and selected object are always visible.
Small terminal sizes degrade gracefully.

tasks.title: Shared TUI view-state model implement
acceptance_criteria: Selected goal/task/artifact/context can be shared across sections.
Refresh state, filters and last selected objects are tracked predictably.
No stale object is silently treated as current.
View-state model is unit tested.

tasks.title: Browser fallback and deep-link helper implement
acceptance_criteria: Every complex object can expose an open-in-browser link where backend/frontend supports it.
TUI clearly marks browser-first operations.
Fallback is available for deep admin, rich artifact rendering and risky config workflows.
Fallback helper is tested.

tasks.title: Dashboard read model view implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI consumes the same dashboard/assistant read models used by frontend where available.
View shows health, readiness, active profile/governance, recent tasks and important warnings.
View handles degraded backend state.
View has fixture and live-mode tests.

tasks.title: System health and contracts view implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI shows system health, contracts, agents, stats and stats history where available.
System events stream is supported if terminal runtime can handle it safely; otherwise polling fallback exists.
Unavailable endpoints show explicit degraded state.
View does not crash on partial backend responses.

tasks.title: Config read-only view implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI shows safe config categories including runtime_profile, governance_mode, providers/model summary and benchmark config where available.
Secrets/tokens are redacted.
Dangerous or complex config stays read-only or browser-first.
Config view is permission-aware.

tasks.title: Safe config edit flow implement
acceptance_criteria: Only explicitly safe config fields can be edited from TUI.
Changes show preview/diff before submit.
Backend validation/audit is required for every config write.
Failed writes do not update local assumed state.

tasks.title: Provider and benchmark views implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI can list providers, provider catalog and LLM benchmark summaries.
Benchmark filters are supported where backend supports them.
Provider secrets are never rendered.
Large benchmark output is summarized with detail drilldown.

tasks.title: Goals list and detail views expand
acceptance_criteria: TUI lists goals with status, team, mode and summary.
Goal detail shows plan, governance summary, trace and related tasks/artifacts where available.
Goal modes are listable and selectable for new goals.
Create goal flow supports explicit context and mode data.

tasks.title: Goal plan tree view implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI can display goal plan nodes/tree in readable terminal form.
Plan node patching is either safely supported or browser-first.
Node state and dependencies are visible.
Malformed plans degrade to raw readable text.

tasks.title: Task workbench view expand
acceptance_criteria: TUI supports task list filters by status/team/agent/error-only where backend supports them.
Task detail shows status, owner/agent, proposal, execution state, artifacts, logs and timeline references.
Task logs can be viewed and optionally streamed or refreshed.
Task patch/assign/propose/execute actions are guarded and confirmation-based.

tasks.title: Task orchestration view implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI shows orchestration read model and worker/task flow state.
Ingest/claim/complete orchestration operations are read-only or guarded by explicit permission and confirmation.
View distinguishes normal, blocked, failed and stale states.
No orchestration operation is performed implicitly.

tasks.title: Archived task management view implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI can list archived tasks.
Restore/cleanup/delete archived task actions require confirmation.
Bulk cleanup shows impact summary before submit.
Browser fallback exists for risky cleanup operations.

tasks.title: Artifact explorer expand
acceptance_criteria: TUI lists artifacts and shows artifact detail.
Extract/index/RAG status/RAG preview are supported where backend supports them.
Large text artifacts have paging or summary mode.
Binary/rich artifacts use browser fallback.

tasks.title: Knowledge collections view implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI can list knowledge collections and index profiles.
TUI can inspect a collection and trigger indexing where safe.
Knowledge search works with query/top_k.
Results show source/provenance where available.

tasks.title: Template management view implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI can list templates and inspect template detail.
Template validation, diagnostics and preview are supported.
Create/update/delete are either browser-first or guarded with confirmation.
Variable registry and sample contexts can be viewed.

tasks.title: Artifact upload strategy decide
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines whether TUI supports artifact upload directly.
If supported, upload path validates file path, size and collection name.
If deferred, TUI clearly links to browser fallback.
No accidental file upload can happen from selection/navigation alone.

tasks.title: Teams and blueprint catalog views implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI can list teams, blueprints, blueprint catalog, team types and roles.
TUI can inspect blueprint detail and team composition.
Instantiate/create/update/delete actions are guarded or browser-first.
Activate team action requires confirmation.

tasks.title: Instruction layer workbench view implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI can show instruction layer model and effective instruction stack.
Profiles and overlays can be listed and inspected.
Selection/attachment actions are explicit and permission-aware.
Governance/safety layers are visibly non-overridable.

tasks.title: Instruction profile and overlay editing strategy implement
acceptance_criteria: Editing profiles/overlays is either supported with preview/validation or kept browser-first.
Attach/detach/select operations require explicit confirmation.
Task/goal instruction selection is visible and auditable.
Invalid instruction input is rejected before submit when possible.

tasks.title: Reference profile placeholder integration prepare
acceptance_criteria: TUI has a placeholder/read-only section for future reference profiles if backend exposes them.
Section does not block current TUI work.
No fake reference profile data is shown.
Browser fallback exists until ref-profile integration is real.

tasks.title: Automation and autopilot views implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI shows autopilot status, auto-planner status and triggers status.
Start/stop/tick autopilot actions are backend-gated and confirmation-based.
Configure auto-planner/triggers is guarded or browser-first.
Security level and concurrency/budget fields are shown clearly.

tasks.title: Audit log explorer implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI lists audit logs with pagination.
Audit analyze action is available where backend supports it.
Sensitive values are redacted.
Audit entries link to task/goal/artifact/trace references where possible.

tasks.title: Approval and proposal review queue implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI shows tasks/proposals requiring review or approval.
Review/approve/reject actions call existing backend APIs only.
Every action shows summary, risk and confirmation before submit.
Stale/denied/already-handled states are handled.

tasks.title: Repair operator view deepen
acceptance_criteria: TUI shows repair diagnosis, proposed steps, dry-run status, approval state, execution result and verification result where backend exposes them.
Repair execution is never implicit.
Unsafe or unknown repair actions are blocked visibly.
Browser fallback exists for complex repair session detail.

tasks.title: Event stream and live refresh model implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI supports manual refresh everywhere.
Optional live refresh/streaming is available for system events/task logs if stable.
Live refresh is rate-limited and stoppable.
Connection loss is visible and recoverable.

tasks.title: TUI view-model test suite expand
acceptance_criteria: View-model tests cover every major TUI section.
Tests include empty, normal, degraded and permission-denied states.
Tests use fixture transports and do not require live backend.
Tests cover dangerous action confirmation logic.

tasks.title: TUI API contract tests expand
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify TUI client expectations against backend response shapes.
Tests cover config, dashboard, tasks, goals, artifacts, knowledge, system, teams, automation and audit APIs.
Breaking API changes produce clear test failures.
Tests do not duplicate frontend TypeScript logic blindly.

tasks.title: TUI golden path smoke suite add
acceptance_criteria: Smoke suite covers dashboard -> goal -> task -> artifact.
Smoke suite covers config read-only, audit list, automation status and repair/approval visibility.
Smoke suite can run against fixture backend.
Smoke suite is part of release gate or clearly documented as manual.

tasks.title: TUI user and operator docs update
acceptance_criteria: Docs list available sections and keyboard shortcuts.
Docs explain safe write actions and browser fallback.
Docs show start command and fixture/live modes.
Docs clearly state what is not yet supported.

tasks.title: TUI frontend-parity readiness report generate
acceptance_criteria: Report maps Angular frontend areas to TUI support status.
Report marks each area as supported, partial, browser-fallback or deferred.
Report lists remaining backend/API gaps if any.
Report separates merge blockers from future polish.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Map Angular API surface to terminal-appropriate TUI sections.
Stage 2: Expand navigation, dashboard, system and config.
Stage 4: Add knowledge, templates, teams, blueprints and instruction layers.
Stage 5: Add automation, audit, approvals and repair views.
Stage 6: Add tests, docs and parity/readiness report.
summary_notes: This track assumes the existing TUI MVP is already runnable.
The goal is broad frontend parity where terminal UX makes sense, not a pixel-for-pixel clone of Angular.
Dangerous write/admin operations must remain confirmation-based, backend-gated and auditable.
Browser fallback is preferred over unsafe or awkward terminal implementations.
